name: Judo_Mech

infobox.Systems: Vice Fist(Prime)EffectGrab a unit and toss it behind you.(1 damage)Upgrade IAlly Immune ()Deals no damage to allies.Upgrade II+2 Damage ()Increases damage by 2.
infobox.Special: Natural Armor: Weapon damage to this unit is reduced by 1. All other damage (Push, Blocking, Fire, etc.) is unaffected.

sections.Team: The mech is available as part of theSteel Judokasquad.
sections.Strategy:
The Judo Mech is useful for moving Vek away from buildings and preventing any damage done to them, as well as completely redirecting attacks against other enemies. With it's additional armor, it can be used to block attacks altogether. The pilotAbe Isamu'sability is wasted on this mech.
Investing in more Mech movement early on is recommended for the Judo Mech; since the Vice Fist weapon needs an unoccupied tile behind the Mech to be able to fire, more movement will allow the Judo Mech more flexibility for where it wants to throw enemies. In particular, look for tiles that can activate environment effects, such as aForest Tile; the damage from Vice Fist will trigger the effect on the tile as well. Pilot abilities that enable better positioning, such as those fromHenry Kwan, are also beneficial for the same reason.
Upgrading Vice Fist is generally not a priority. TheAlly Immuneupgrade is extremely situational to be worth it; in theory, it could help with repositioning ally units away from harm, but in practice, it is usually better to reposition the enemies themselves. The+2 Damageupgrade could help it kill an enemy, if throwing enemies intoA.C.I.D. Pools; this is more useful if using the Judo Mech in aCustom Squadthat can consistently provide A.C.I.D. though. In the Steel Judoka, it is not always beneficial to kill enemies with direct damage, as repositioning them into other enemy attacks can often be sufficient thanks toVek Hormones.
Regardless of where this Mech is used, Vice Fist can only target enemies if there's space behind the Judo Mech to throw them, and is still completely unable to damage enemies in tight corners. Additionally, it is unable to target stable units, most notablyBurrowers. To circumvent this weakness, it's recommended to invest on a secondary weapon that can deal direct damage without grabbing the enemy. The pilotKazaaakplethcan be alternatively equipped on the Judo Mech to provide this through his ability.
In a Custom Squad, the Judo Mech may be used with theLeap Mechas well, and have them swap their weapons around; the innate armor for the Judo Mech will prevent the initial self-damage fromHydraulic Legs. This does, however, leave the Leap Mech with the less useful Vice Fist weapon, so consider equipping Kazaaakpleth orHarold Schmidton the Leap Mech if trying this combination for a Custom Squad.
